The graph-representing strategy of structural knowledge in the entorhinal-hippocampal system motivates the spatiotemporal embedding learning in a graph-based unsupervised learning manner.
The spatiotemporal embedding learning is solved in a graph-based unsupervised learning manner through specific context graph-building policies.
The conjunctive representation mechanism in the entorhinal-hippocampal complex inspires the spatiotemporal model with a grid-cell spatial encoder and a time pattern encoder.
The spatiotemporal model utilizes a grid-cell spatial encoder as part of its structure.
The conjunctive representing approach based on a unique spatiotemporal context graph addresses the problem of previous spatiotemporal modeling methods.

A successive POI recommendation system is introduced by incorporating STEP and simple sequence predictors to show the feasibility of implementing specific applications based on the proposed STE framework.

To demonstrate the generalizability of STE
The paper proposes the spatiotemporal embedding framework STE and applies it to POIs (STEP).
This work translates entorhinal-hippocampal representing mechanisms to the spatiotemporal embedding.
The framework STE captures sequential and spatiotemporal representation from unlabeled sparse data through context graph building and graph-based embedding learning
